raw_responses.query: RAG retrieval relevance Zipf power law scaling LLM 2025 arxiv

results.title: Relevance Isn't All You Need: Scaling RAG Systems With Inference-Time Compute Via Multi-Criteria Reranking
results.snippet: Existing RAG systems scale poorly with inference time compute; introduces REBEL multi-criteria optimization scaling for RAG.

results.title: A Systematic Review of Key Retrieval-Augmented Generation Systems
results.snippet: RAG systems evolution and state-of-the-art implementations survey.

results.title: RAG Evaluation in the Era of LLMs: A Comprehensive Survey
results.snippet: Trend of increasing application of LLM-based evaluation methods for RAG.

results.title: RAG in the Wild: (In)effectiveness of LLMs with retrieval
results.snippet: Studies effectiveness of LLMs with RAG in production conditions.

raw_responses.query: "retrieval relevance" power law "k^-" OR "Zipf" scaling exponent LLM 2025

results.title: AlphaZero Neural Scaling and Zipf's Law: a Tale of Board Games and Power Laws
results.snippet: AlphaZero agents exhibit scaling laws that produce train and test data following Zipf's law; correlation between Zipf curve and scaling exponents. Quantization model proposes power-law scaling in LLMs may arise because LLMs learn discrete task quanta whose frequency follows Zipf's law.

results.title: Zipf's and Heaps' Laws for Tokens and LLM-generated Texts
results.snippet: Zipf-Mandelbrot-like distributions in LLM-generated text token frequencies.

results.title: Random Text, Zipf's Law, Critical Length, and Implications for LLMs
results.snippet: Random text simulations demonstrating Zipf with implications for LLM token distribution.

results.title: Power Law Distributions in Information Retrieval
results.snippet: Several properties of IR data — query frequency, document length — are widely considered approximately distributed as power law; focus on scale-free / long-tail nature of empirical distributions.

results.title: How Do LLM-Generated Texts Impact Term-Based Retrieval Models?
results.snippet: Study of how LLM-generated text affects term-based retrieval performance.
